# CURATED SGLang v0.5.13 server-option reference excerpts; not captured --help output.
# Sources:
# https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/v0.5.13/docs/advanced_features/server_arguments.md
# https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/v0.5.13/docs/advanced_features/deterministic_inference.md
# Retrieval: option names and adjacent descriptions, retrieved 2026-07-11.

--model-path
The path of the model weights. This can be a local folder or a Hugging Face repo ID.
--tokenizer-path
The path of the tokenizer.
--served-model-name
Override the model name returned by the v1/models endpoint in OpenAI API server.
--host
The host of the HTTP server.
--port
The port of the HTTP server.
--api-key
Set API key of the server. It is also used in the OpenAI API compatible server.
--context-length
The model's maximum context length.
--max-running-requests
The maximum number of running requests.
--random-seed
The random seed.
--dtype
Data type for model weights and activations.
--kv-cache-dtype
Data type for kv cache storage.
--mamba-ssm-dtype
The data type of the SSM states in mamba cache.
--quantization
The quantization method.
--mem-fraction-static
The fraction of the memory used for static allocation (model weights and KV cache memory pool).
--load-format
The format of the model weights to load.
--chat-template
The builtin chat template name or the path of the chat template file.
--sampling-defaults
Where to get default sampling parameters.
--device
The device to use ('cuda', 'xpu', 'hpu', 'npu', 'cpu').
--tp-size
The tensor parallelism size.
--dp-size
The data parallelism size.
--chunked-prefill-size
The maximum number of tokens in a chunk for the chunked prefill. Setting this to -1 means disabling chunked prefill.
--disable-radix-cache
Disable RadixAttention for prefix caching.
--disable-overlap-schedule
Disable the overlap scheduler.
--cuda-graph-max-bs
The maximum batch size to capture by CUDA Graph.
--attention-backend
Choose attention backend (flashinfer, fa3, or triton).
--enable-deterministic-inference
Enable deterministic inference with batch-invariant operations.
